headline: Bam Azizi, Mesh CEO: A Fortt Knox Conversation
summary: Mesh CEO Bam Azizi told Jon Fortt his thesis: crypto is "built by crypto bros for crypto bros" and doesn't pass the grandma test. Mesh — fresh off a $75M Series C at $1B led by Dragonfly — abstracts wallet/chain fragmentation so users tap-and-pay. Born in Iran during the Iran-Iraq war, PhD from TU Munich, sold prior startup NoPassword to LogMeIn (2019). Mesh pivoted from B2C to B2B in 2022, post-FTX. Big bet: agentic commerce + microtransactions.

Big bet: agentic commerce + microtransactions.

headline: How Recent Shifts Are Rewriting The Story For Visa (V)
summary: The latest analyst update nudged Visa model fair value estimate from US$396.83 to US$395.71, signaling only a very small adjustment to where the shares are pegged in updated work. That modest shift lines up with a research backdrop where some firms are lifting targets or highlighting Visa as a core payments holding, while others are trimming price targets and stressing valuation, growth resilience, regulation and new payment options. As you read on, you will see how these crosscurrents are...
